textPlain: NFS is the backbone of my home network servers, including file sharing (books, movies, music), local backups, source code and development, and large volumes of data for hobby projects. I don't know what I'd do without it. Haven't found anything more suitable in 15+ years.
What are most people using today for file serving?  For our little lan sftp seems adequate, since ssh is already running.
We are still using it for some pretty large apps. Still have not found a good and simple alternative. I like the simplicity and performance. Scaling is a challenge though.

Unfortunately there doesn’t seem to be any decent alternative.SMB is a nightmare to set up if your host isn’t running Windows.sshfs is actually pretty good but it’s not exactly ubiquitous. Plus it has its own quirks and performs slower. So it really doesn’t feel like an upgrade.Everything else I know of is either proprietary, or hard to set up. Or both.These days everything has gone more cloud-oriented. Eg Dropbox et al. And I don’t want to sync with a cloud server just to sync between two local machines.

True. But for example a home server I absolutely love the simplicity. I have 6 Lenovo 720q machines, one of them as a data storage just running simple NFS for quick daily backups before it pushes them to a NAS.
